pi_items.source: setup_task.sh explicitly copies the file to this exact path.
export_result.sh checks for this exact path for geometry extraction.
verifier.py explicitly checks if the modified geometry has a cylindrical face with a diameter of exactly 16.0mm.
verifier.py explicitly checks if the modified geometry has at least 4 cylindrical faces with a diameter of 5.5mm.
verifier.py extracts this value as `tol` and uses it directly in diameter matching: `abs(d - target_bore) <= tol`.
This parameter is completely ignored by verifier.py. The verifier only performs a loose bound check (ensuring max dimension is strictly between 10 and 500) rather than a rigid 5% tolerance check.

privileged_info_summary: The task involves a real FreeCAD model (T8_housing_bracket.FCStd) created by FreeCAD contributor @hasecilu. The input file path (/home/ga/Documents/FreeCAD/T8_housing_bracket.FCStd) and output file path (/home/ga/Documents/FreeCAD/modified_housing.FCStd) are strictly enforced by the setup and export scripts. The target bore diameter (16.0mm) and target mounting hole diameter (5.5mm) are correctly specified and actively verified by the geometry analysis in export_result.sh and verifier.py using a 0.1mm tolerance. However, the claimed bbox_tolerance_percent of 5% is contradicted because it is entirely ignored; the verifier only ensures the part's maximum bounding box dimension remains broadly between 10 and 500 mm to check if the part was deleted or corrupted.
